rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC 2018 format XX-XXXX (two digits, hyphen, four digits). Any deviation indicates a parsing or type-coercion failure in the ingestor.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All 10 sample values match ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ (XX-XXXX format). No trailing decimals, no missing hyphens.' Domain context: 'SOC code format: XX-XXXX — every soc_code must match.' Threshold: 0 violations (100% conformance).

rules.name: SOC code is not a summary code (no XX-0000)
rules.description: Summary SOC codes (XX-0000) represent major group aggregates, not individual occupations. The ingestor must filter these out. Any summary code in the output means the ingestor filter is broken.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'The sample XLSX contains 1 summary row (SOC 11-0000) which the ingestor correctly filters via soc_digits.endswith("0000") check.' Domain context: 'Summary rows must be filtered by the ingestor. ~22 expected in raw XLSX.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.bls_ooh GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per detailed occupation (soc_code). Any duplicate soc_code indicates an ingestor dedup failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: '10 distinct SOC codes across 10 rows, zero duplicates.' Grain defined in spec as [soc_code]. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: Wage cap consistency: median_wage_capped=true IFF median_annual_wage=239200.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE (median_wage_capped = true AND (median_annual_wage IS NULL OR median_annual_wage != 239200.0)) OR (median_wage_capped = false AND median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL AND median_annual_wage = 239200.0) OR (median_annual_wage IS NULL AND median_wage_capped = true)
rules.description: The median_wage_capped flag must be true if and only if median_annual_wage equals 239200.0 (the BLS wage cap). When wage is null (N/A occupations), capped must be false. Any inconsistency is an ingestor bug in wage parsing.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'The single True value (Surgeons, 29-1248) has wage exactly $239,200. The null-wage row (Legislators) correctly has capped=false. Consistency is perfect.' Domain context: 'Enforce consistency rule: median_wage_capped=true IFF median_annual_wage=239200.0. This is a HARD rejection rule.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Education code range: 1-8
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE education_code < 1 OR education_code > 8
rules.description: BLS education level classification defines exactly 8 levels (1=Doctoral or professional degree through 8=No formal educational credential). Values outside 1-8 indicate data corruption or source schema change.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Expected range: 1-8 in full dataset. Sample shows codes 3, 6, 8.' Domain context: 'Integer codes 1-8 mapping to entry-level education requirements.' BLS documentation defines exactly 8 levels. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work experience code range: 1-3
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE work_experience_code < 1 OR work_experience_code > 3
rules.description: BLS work experience classification defines exactly 3 levels (1=5 years or more, 2=Less than 5 years, 3=None). Values outside 1-3 indicate data corruption.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Expected range: 1-3 in full dataset. Sample shows codes 1, 3.' Domain context: 'Integer codes 1-3: 1=None, 2=Less than 5 years, 3=5 years or more.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Training code range: 1-6
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE training_code < 1 OR training_code > 6
rules.description: BLS training classification defines exactly 6 levels (1=Internship/residency, 2=Apprenticeship, 3=Long-term OJT, 4=Moderate-term OJT, 5=Short-term OJT, 6=None). Values outside 1-6 indicate data corruption.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Full range is 1-6. Sample shows codes 1, 2, 3, 4.' Domain context: 'Integer codes 1-6.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Median wage null rate below 5%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(null_count AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) > 0.05 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN median_annual_wage IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS null_count FROM raw.bls_ooh)
rules.description: Median wage is null for N/A occupations (elected officials, self-employed-dominated). EDA sample shows 10% null but that over-represents N/A occupations. Full dataset expected 2-4%. Threshold set at 5% per EDA recommendation.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 10.0% (1 of 10 rows) — the null is Legislators (SOC 11-1031).' EDA recommendation: 'Set null threshold at 5% for full dataset. Expect ~20-30 of ~832 occupations to have N/A wages.' Domain context: 'N/A wage — expected ~20-30 of ~832 occupations (~2-4%).' Threshold: null rate <= 5%.

rules.name: Occupation title completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE occupation_title IS NULL
rules.description: Every occupation must have a title. The spec marks occupation_title as required. EDA shows 0% null in sample.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 10 rows). Cardinality: 10 distinct values (100% uniqueness — 1:1 with soc_code).' Spec schema: occupation_title is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 750-900
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 750 OR cnt > 900 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.bls_ooh)
rules.description: BLS documents ~832 detailed occupations. The post-filter row count should be 750-900, allowing ~10% variation across projection cycles. A count outside this range suggests a download failure, filter error, or source format change. Note: this threshold should be relaxed during development with sample data.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 10 (sample; full BLS dataset expected ~832 detailed occupations).' EDA recommendation: 'Total row count: 750-900. Allow ~10% variation across projection cycles.' Domain context: '~832 detailed occupations.' Threshold: 750 <= count <= 900.

rules.name: Employment current must be positive (when non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE employment_current IS NOT NULL AND employment_current <= 0
rules.description: A detailed occupation in the BLS projections must have positive current employment. Zero or negative current employment is domain-impossible.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Min: 36,600 (Surgeons). All 10 rows have positive values.' Domain context: 'Employment values must be positive — a detailed occupation with zero or negative employment would not be in the projections.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Employment projected must be positive (when non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE employment_projected IS NOT NULL AND employment_projected <= 0
rules.description: A detailed occupation in the BLS projections must have positive projected employment. Even sharply declining occupations are projected to retain some workers over a 10-year horizon.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Min: 38,500. All 10 rows have positive values.' Domain context: 'employment_projected must be > 0 for detailed occupations.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Openings annual avg must be positive (when non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE openings_annual_avg IS NOT NULL AND openings_annual_avg < 0
rules.description: Annual average openings must be non-negative. Zero is permitted for very small occupations where BLS source values round to 0 from thousands (e.g., occupations with <50 annual openings). Negative values are domain-impossible.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Min: 2,100 (Surgeons). All 10 rows have positive values.' Domain context: 'openings_annual_avg is ALWAYS positive. Declining occupations still have openings due to replacement needs.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: soc_code completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: soc_code is the required grain field. Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 10 rows) — required grain field.' Spec schema: soc_code is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: median_wage_capped completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE median_wage_capped IS NULL
rules.description: median_wage_capped is a required boolean field. Must be populated for every row (false when wage is null, true when wage is capped).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0.0% (0 of 10 rows).' Spec schema: median_wage_capped is required (yes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Median wage range: $20,000-$239,200 (when non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE median_annual_wage IS NOT NULL AND (median_annual_wage < 20000 OR median_annual_wage > 239200)
rules.description: When non-null, median wage should be between $20,000 (approximate federal minimum wage full-time floor) and $239,200 (BLS wage cap ceiling). Values outside this range indicate a parsing error.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Min: $33,530 (Home health aides), Max: $239,200 (Surgeons, capped).' Domain context: 'Wage range $20,000-$239,200. No BLS occupation should have median below ~$20k. Max is the BLS cap.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: load_date within last 30 days (freshness)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE load_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30' DAY LIMIT 1
rules.description: The load_date should be recent (within 30 days of query execution). Stale data suggests the pipeline has not been refreshed.
rules.evidence: Consistent with existing College Scorecard freshness rule (RAW-CS-017). All rows share the same load_date from a single batch load. Threshold: 0 violations within 30-day window.

rules.name: Employment change consistency: projected - current = change (within rounding tolerance)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.bls_ooh WHERE employment_current IS NOT NULL AND employment_projected IS NOT NULL AND employment_change IS NOT NULL AND ABS(employment_projected - employment_current - employment_change) > 1000
rules.description: employment_change should equal employment_projected minus employment_current, within a +/- 1000 rounding tolerance (from the thousands-to-actual conversion where raw values like 1795.5 are multiplied by 1000 and rounded to long).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'For all 10 rows: employment_projected - employment_current = employment_change (verified to within rounding tolerance from thousands conversion).' Domain context: 'Employment change consistency rule should allow +/- 1000 tolerance.' Threshold: 0 violations outside tolerance.
